title: DIDComm interop fixture (v1)
description: Canonical manifest format for the didcomm-dotnet interop fixture suite. One manifest per fixture; the InteropTests runner enumerates manifest/**/*.json and emits one xUnit theory case per file. See PRD §13.4.

schema.description: Schema discriminator. Always 'didcomm-fixture/v1' for this version.

id.description: Stable, unique identifier for the fixture. Used to attribute test failures to a specific case.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what the fixture exercises.

source.description: Provenance tag identifying where the fixture originated. PRD §13.2 enumerates the canonical values: 'spec-v2.1', 'didcomm-rust', 'didcomm-python', 'didcomm-jvm', 'didcomm-rs', 'credo-ts', 'authored', or 'interop-a-thon-<date>'.

source_ref.description: Pointer into the source — e.g. 'Appendix C.3 example 1' or a commit-pinned file path.

direction.description: inbound = didcomm-dotnet consumes a foreign-produced artifact; outbound = a foreign impl consumes ours; roundtrip = pack→unpack against ourselves.

operation.description: The DIDComm operation under test. 'noop' is reserved for runner smoke tests that exist only to prove the runner enumerates the file.

refs.description: Pointers from the manifest into the sibling fixture data directories.

input.description: Operation inputs. For unpack/verify: 'packed' points at the envelope. For pack-*: carries message, from, to[], sign_from?, enc, protect_sender?, forward?.

error_code.description: When outcome=error, the expected error code or exception type.

plaintext.description: Path to the expected plaintext payload (for unpack).

match.description: exact = byte-equal output (deterministic algs only); structural = same recipients/headers/alg, ephemerals ignored; roundtrip = pack-then-unpack equals source plaintext.
